summary: Static packet for testing immigration-policy role behavior. The packet is intentionally compact and requires models to caveat current legal details.

sources.title: USCIS official immigration forms and case tools
claims: US immigration filings, forms, fees, and eligibility details should be verified through USCIS or other official agency pages.
Immigration rules vary by status, filing type, country, date, and jurisdiction.
sources.notes: Use for official-source traceability, not as a live legal determination.

sources.title: Department of Homeland Security immigration enforcement and border information
claims: Federal immigration policy involves border management, lawful pathways, enforcement, humanitarian protection, and agency implementation.
Current enforcement statistics and policy changes require dated official verification.
sources.notes: Packet does not include live enforcement totals.

sources.title: Bipartisan immigration-policy issue frames
claims: Common enforcement-focused frames include border capacity, rule of law, labor-market effects, public services, and security.
Common protection-focused frames include asylum access, family unity, humanitarian obligations, due process, and economic contribution.
